Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-24 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's data shows two exceptional performers at the top of the momentum rankings, with TNGX (#1, score 95.9) and PARR (#2) displaying starkly different risk profiles. TNGX exhibits extraordinary long-term returns (998.58% over 1 year) with exceptional consistency across all timeframes and perfect SMA positioning, but its extreme ratios and massive gap between near-term and long-term gains suggest it may be a highly volatile, low-priced stock riding a sustained uptrend rather than showing balanced short-term momentum. PARR, by contrast, shows more measured but still impressive gains (330.85% 1-year return) with better ratio stability and notably stronger outperformance on down-SPY days (bad_spy_score of 2.62 vs TNGX's 0.38), indicating it has defensive qualities that could provide resilience. Both pass all SMA gates and significantly outpace major indices, but TNGX's extreme skew toward the 1-month-to-1-year timeframe versus PARR's more balanced accumulation pattern suggests different underlying drivers, with TNGX looking like a potential momentum exhaustion candidate despite its top ranking while PARR appears more structurally sound.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
